_doc: Schema 4 search_plan_handoff shape — input to the handoff adapter. Produced by buildSearchPlan() in searchPlanBuilder.js, then enriched by runDiscoverySeedPlan.js with planner/learning/panel metadata. Updated 2026-03-16.

queries.query_hash: string — deterministic hash for dedup across rounds
queries.family: string — query family: manufacturer_html | manual_pdf | review_lookup | benchmark_lookup | support_docs | fallback_web | targeted_single
queries.group_key: string — field group this query targets (e.g. sensor_performance, physical)
target_fields: string — field keys this query is expected to resolve
preferred_domains: string — domains to prefer for this query (used as domain_hint)
queries.exact_match_required: boolean — whether results must exact-match the product identity

query_hashes: string — flat array of all query_hash values for dedup

_metadata_enrichment._doc: runDiscoverySeedPlan.js attaches these underscore-prefixed fields to the handoff object after Schema 4 completes. They are NOT part of the buildSearchPlan() output — they are added at the orchestration layer so searchDiscovery.js can thread them into searchProfilePlanned.

_planner.model: string — LLM model used for planning
_planner.planner_complete: boolean — whether the planner finished successfully
_planner.planner_confidence: number — overall plan confidence 0-1 from LLM response
_planner.targeted_exceptions: number — exceptional queries added outside normal rules
_planner.error: string | null — error message if mode is 'error'

query_hashes_generated: string — hashes of all generated queries
queries_generated: string — raw query text of all generated queries
families_used: string — query families represented in the plan
domains_targeted: string — preferred domains across all queries
groups_activated: string — field groups that received queries

_panel.bundles: Array<{ key, label, desc, priority, phase, source_target, content_target, search_intent, host_class, query_family_mix, reason_active, queries[], fields[] }>
_panel.deltas: Array<{ field, from, to }> — round-over-round changes
